title: Psychoanalyzing Asukas Doll
wikitext:
[]: Psychoanalyzing Asuka's Doll             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 06, 2013 5:56 pm [Split from "The Moral of Rebuild" . - Monk] Conspicuous wrote: Honestly, I'd say that the visuals involving all the damage to her plugsuit makes it pretty clear that Asuka is hiding some significant emotional scars, as if she didn't have enough of those already .

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Sep 06, 2013 7:29 pm That's an interesting interpretation of Asuka's doll in 2.0 and the final shot of it in the plastic box. Even if we didn't saw more of Asuka until after the time skip, it's obvious that her wake up must have been very rude : first her left eye is dead, then on top of that said left eye had an angelic contamination which probably led to a quarantine cell with lots of probing and experimentations, on top of that she learn that Shinji and Rei disappeared absorbed by EVA-01 and that Tokyo-3 was destroyed as a consequence, and if she woke up even later it's possible that it was after the Actual 3I and that she was greeted by a Tokyo-3 reduced to a giant wasteland of core material(and maybe even the rest of the world! ), oh and some random chick with glasses stole her Eva and trashed it! All of this would effectively make the Bardiel incident the end of her innocence and "childhood"(as much of being trained to pilot fight since age 4 could be called a childhood), symbolized by the doll in the plastic box, and right after Asuka confessed to Misato that having friends and being with them is not so bad and the later telling her to look forward all the experiences she still has to live, just to make the scene even more gut-wrenching...a chance that at least she met Mari and become good friend with her, if not I don't dare to imagine the enraged mental wreck she would be in Q!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 06, 2013 7:54 pm ElMariachi wrote: All of this would effectively make the Bardiel incident the end of her innocence and "childhood"(as much of being trained to pilot fight since age 4 could be called a childhood), symbolized by the doll in the plastic box, and right after Asuka confessed to Misato that having friends and being with them is not so bad and the later telling her to look forward all the experiences she still has to live, just to make the scene even more gut-wrenching...a chance that at least she met Mari and become good friend with her, if not I don't dare to imagine the enraged mental wreck she would be in Q!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Sep 07, 2013 7:03 am Wait, Asuka only had one conversation with her doll in Ha, and she wasn't ordering it, where do you see the similarities with Asuka giving Mari orders in Q? Same thing for your second point : what are the points of Kyoko's story that were pasted onto Asuka's?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 07, 2013 8:18 am ElMariachi wrote: Wait, Asuka only had one conversation with her doll in Ha, and she wasn't ordering it, where do you see the similarities with Asuka giving Mari orders in Q?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Sep 09, 2013 4:51 am I hope the topic title I chose accurately conveys the specificity of this topic. This is not just about what Asuka's doll "means", but about what it means for Asuka's psychology . If the topic broadens significantly from there, the title can always be changed to something more general. S'what happened to this thing's parent topic, after all.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Sep 09, 2013 8:37 am @ Freaky : I think you may have been over-analyzing Asuka's relationship with her doll. For you the doll represent the "childish" part of Asuka's personality, that she consciously externalize through said doll to "subdue" it, illustrated when she "interrupt" the doll. But the doll is actually agreeing with Asuka, and even has the same elitist personality that the real Asuka : "I'm not like them", "I'm special", that's exactly how the Asuka we see in public behaves, so the doll doesn't represent Asuka's repressed childish and bubbly aspects, since it thinks exactly like "Captain" Asuka. For me the doll is just a way to represent the fact that Asuka doesn't have any friends, and cope with it by creating one, but since she can only count on herself by her own words, said friend is just herself in another form. That's also why she said the last phrase with her own voice instead of the "doll voice", it was to show us that Asuka isn't delusional to the point that she truly think that the doll is alive, she said the last line herself to reveal to us that Asuka knows that she's just talking to herself. As for her relationship with Mari, it's actually the exact contrary as with the doll : the doll is a "friend" made of her image that never contradict her, since it's Asuka who's putting its words in its mouth, while Mari is a living being, with her own personality and thoughts, and more importantly she talks back to Asuka! - Asuka : Your support fire was 2 seconds late! - Mari : And you're three seconds ahead of position. And even if Asuka tells her to adapt, the tone in which Mari answers(the "As you wish, Princess!") was clearly snarky and not subservient, for Mari it's obviously Asuka who is having another of her fits(why would have she nicknamed Asuka "Princess" if not) - Mari : How's Puppy-Boy? - Asuka : The same as always, with the same lowly look on his face! - Mari : So you've gone only to see his face? *Asuka shoots Giant Rei Head Fetus Thing* - Mari : Don't waste your bullets Princess , the whole thing is a core! If a link must be make between Asuka's interaction with doll and with Mari, I would say that the dolls represented Asuka's unwillingness to make real friends(she rejected everyone who talked to her in class, was always playing with her console when alone more than ten seconds, and didn't become immediately friend with Hikari(although it's her name that first came in Asuka's mind when she tried to lie to Misato about for who she was cooking, implying that they where starting a friendship)), and to compensate created her own "friend" that would never disagree with her, since said "friend" is a doll voiced by Asuka herself. But in Q Asuka talking with Mari, and even tolerating when she steps out of her bounds(the comment about why she had gone to see Shinji) means that she accepted the closeness of another person, a real person and not an inanimate substitute. Of course since we don't know anything in Q about characters who are not called Shinji(thanks of the extremely centric POV) and Rei Q(because there is almost nothing, and we saw the little there was to see), we can't say if Asuka's interaction with Mari means that she's now comfortable with dealing with other people(who aren't called Shinji), or if she's actually only willing to get close and interacts with Mari because of their similar situation(Eva pilots, Curse of Eva, teammates) and still is as much as a shut-in with everyone else than 14 years ago. That would put Mari's line toward Shinji "at least go help the princess!" under a new light : Mari could have the hope that since Shinji has too some similarities with Asuka(Eva pilot, probably the Curse of Eva too) and thus could enlarge Asuka's circle of friends, which would do her some good, especially since Shinji know her from before the Bardiel incident and got profoundly modified by his experience as a pilot too (Impact Trigger for him, Funky glowing eye for her), something that Mari can't relate to, since she "only" got the Curse of Eva so far. Needless to say, by the end of Q things are not looking very promising...

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Sep 09, 2013 12:25 pm Note that it's a puppet , not a doll. The difference is significant. A person controls a puppet more than a doll (which is inert), but controls the puppet by inserting part of the body into it. Hands in Evangelion represent the self, and in manipulating the puppet, it covers up her hand. The conversation she has with the puppet is centered on reinforcing her beliefs and behaviors. The puppet is in effect a voodoo doll she uses to manipulate herself, and is symbolic of the false persona she's constructed to separate herself from other people.

[]:             Quote Post by Conspicuous » Mon Sep 09, 2013 1:15 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Hm. Maybe "subservient" is a better way to put it, though the relationship between Asuka and her doll is an odd one. Sure, Asuka's name being on the doll probably means that, well, it's her doll. Her parents were probably rich (she's has a LOT of stuff) so getting a doll costume embroidered probably wasn't a big deal. Or it could be that one of her parents knew how to sew. Either way, the point is that Asuka's name on the doll is probably there to clarify ownership of the doll.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Sep 09, 2013 1:41 pm Conspicuous wrote: Either way, the fact that we don't see it any more in 3.0 speaks volumes. At this point, it's clear that Asuka left her childhood aside, for better or for worse. Probably the worse.

[]:             Quote Post by Conspicuous » Mon Sep 09, 2013 1:48 pm That doesn't matter, though. The only things that matter are the things that are shown to us through the film. If they found it important to Asuka's character to retain those elements, they would've shown them to us, but since they didn't, we can safely conclude that they're no longer particularly important to her character at this point. If anything, the film goes out of its way to show that she has nothing left other than piloting Eva. We don't ever see her outside of a plugsuit, even when she's wearing her "casual" clothing, she's still wearing her plugsuit underneath and ready for action at any moment. That's what Anno wanted to show us through the language of film, and so that's what mattered.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Sep 09, 2013 2:06 pm ElMariachi wrote: - Asuka : Your support fire was 2 seconds late! - Mari : And you're three seconds ahead of position. And even if Asuka tells her to adapt, the tone in which Mari answers(the "As you wish, Princess!") was clearly snarky and not subservient, for Mari it's obviously Asuka who is having another of her fits(why would have she nicknamed Asuka "Princess" if not) - Mari : How's Puppy-Boy? - Asuka : The same as always, with the same lowly look on his face! - Mari : So you've gone only to see his face? *Asuka shoots Giant Rei Head Fetus Thing* - Mari : Don't waste your bullets Princess , the whole thing is a core!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Sep 09, 2013 5:12 pm @ Conspicuous : well, the focus of Q wasn't on Asuka, or anybody of WILLE. When you think about the only people who got some character focus were Shinji and Rei Q with her slowing questioning herself, even Kaworu is reduced to a mere plot point gravitating around Shinji(although his case is interesting in that he willingly put himself in this position). All the other characters only appeared when the plot required them, so we never got any scene developing their character or showing what kind of character they became after the time skip. Although I admit that your interpretation of Asuka always wearing her plugsuit is plausible, a way to show us that she became a full-fledged soldier like everyone else who never are out of their uniform(except Ritsuko, strangely, when she take off her coat during Shinji's debriefing), although it could also be because they just got EVA-01 running, and apparently neo-NERV tried more than once to take it from them(actually Shinji in its entry plug) and thus she had to stay in gear for the moment... I guess we will have our answer in FINAL, since the ending of Q have Asuka and Rei Q alone with Shinji, I hope we will get some scenes about the former to see her when not in battle, you know, scenes like Asuka with her puppet, Misato and Ritsuko talking in the bar in Misato's car, or the Bridge Bunny talking between them around a coffee... something to show us that they are still human beings and not lifeless drones, I mean we already Rei Q to show what a human being entirely focused in the purpose assigned by her superior looks like! @ Freaky : Truth be told, there wasn't any other thing to do for Mari than fall in line : she wouldn't refuse to support her just to prove her point! What we need to have is a conversation between the two of them without having a life or death situation above their head(even Mari's comment about Asuka's reason to go to see Shinji was cut short by the fact that Mark.09 was attacking), to see what is the dynamic of their relationship when out of duty(they must be resting sometime, they're not machines, nor ReiQ!), here again, I hope FINAL will give us something.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Sep 11, 2013 9:52 am Note that a) The Doll (In Rebuild) had lots of irregular sticking on it, like it went through some repairing. Possibly lttle Asuka sewing it back together after Kyoko tore it to bits. b) We see NGE!Asuka pick up one of the dolls in episode 25. something interesting I'Ve read, but haven't found further confirmation for is the claim that in some yet untraslated piece of CR, there's a confirmation that: a) this is indeed supposed to be one of Kyoko's dolls and b) Asuka plays with it whenever she feels she's not living up to her facade. Anyone who's read the entire thing here? We don't see the doll in Q, but neither do we see Rei's broken glasses or Misato's cross pendant.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Sep 11, 2013 10:34 am Kendrix wrote: b) Asuka plays with it whenever she feels she's not living up to her facade.

[]:             Quote Post by Lord ikari shinji » Thu Sep 26, 2013 3:57 pm I feel like the Asuka in the rebuild is weakly developed sadly/ that one puppet/doll scene in 2.0 was suppose to be a summary of Asuka mental state and character development from NGE, how pathetic I feel like Asuka shikimani Langley is a shallow imitation of Asuka Langley soryu, shame on to Anno and staff for this poor development in Asuka,Misato{as well}, and mari {must definitely/ she is currently a fodder imo} damn but I still love the new Asuka due to the time ship of 14 years, because now she's a woman and more mature, I look forward to seeing how is will express her feelings towards Shinji who still has the mental state of a child/etc. in 4.0 which is rumored to have a run time 2.5 hours.. sorry for getting abit side track OP/members im trying to say that most mains (Asuka/etc.) in the rebuild are poorly develop
summary:
[]: Psychoanalyzing Asuka's Doll             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 06, 2013 5:56 pm [Split from "The Moral of Rebuild" . - Monk] Conspicuous wrote: Honestly, I'd say that the visuals involving all the damage to her plugsuit makes it pretty clear that Asuka is hiding some significant emotional scars, as if she didn't have enough of those already .

[]:          &n
